comment: System tool catalog — maps tool names to categories and metadata. User custom tools live in ~/.memory/config/tool_catalog.json. Roles declare tool_access by category.

file.description: File read, write, and search operations

browser.description: Headless browser — render pages, interact, screenshot (via Playwright MCP)

terminal.description: Persistent terminal sessions via tmux — run commands, read output

orchestration.description: Agent-to-agent coordination — schedule tasks, hand off work between roles

google.description: Google Workspace — Calendar, Gmail, Drive, Contacts (via Google Workspace MCP or gws CLI)

memory_search.description: Search the user's personal memory — past conversations, archived knowledge, and synthesized insights. Use when the task requires context from prior interactions or stored knowledge.

add_conversation.description: Store a new conversation or piece of information into long-term memory. Use after completing a task to preserve findings, decisions, or context for future recall.

read_file.description: Read the contents of a file from the filesystem. Returns text with line numbers. Use before modifying any file — always read first.

write_file.description: Write or overwrite a file on the filesystem. Only allowed in approved sandbox paths. Prefer making targeted edits when possible; use for creating new files or full rewrites.

list_files.description: List files and directories at a given path. Use to explore project structure before reading or writing.

search_in_files.description: Search for text patterns across multiple files using grep/ripgrep. Use to locate function definitions, variable references, or specific strings across a codebase.

web_search.description: Search the web for current information, documentation, or external context. Use when the task requires up-to-date knowledge not available in memory or local files. Requires GOOGLE_API_KEY and GOOGLE_SEARCH_ENGINE_ID.

fetch_url.description: Fetch and read the full plain-text content of a web page. Use after web_search to read a specific result in depth. Strips HTML — returns readable text.

bash_exec.description: Run one shell command or a short list of shell commands sequentially. Restricted by a destructive-command blocklist and explicit authorization. Use for builds, scripts, or system queries that cannot be done with other tools.

run_tests.description: Run the project's test suite. Use after making code changes to verify correctness. Returns pass/fail output with details on any failures.

scheduler_add_task.description: Schedule a new task for another agent role. Use to hand off work after completing your own step — e.g. a provisioner queuing a reviewer. The new task runs asynchronously; this tool returns immediately without waiting for the result.

dispatch_subtask.description: Dispatch a task to another agent role and WAIT for the result before continuing. Use when you need a specialist's output inline — e.g. a researcher dispatching to a provisioner, then using the provisioner's output to continue. Max depth: 2. Blocked when local_only is set.

ask_user.description: Pause the task and ask the user a question. Use when you are blocked and only the user can unblock you — missing credentials, ambiguous requirements, or a decision that requires human judgment. The task waits in HITL state until the user replies. Do not use this when another tool can answer the question.

find-session.description: Find a tmux session by name.

list-windows.description: List windows in a tmux session.

list-panes.description: List panes in a tmux window.

capture-pane.description: Capture content from a tmux pane with configurable line count.

create-window.description: Create a new window in a tmux session.

kill-session.description: Kill a tmux session by ID.

kill-window.description: Kill a tmux window by ID.

kill-pane.description: Kill a tmux pane by ID.

split-pane.description: Split a tmux pane horizontally or vertically.

execute-command.description: Execute a command in a tmux pane and get results.

get-command-result.description: Get the result of a previously executed tmux command.

browser.description: Control a headless browser — navigate, click, type, fill forms, take screenshots, and read page state. Pass action + params. See action enum in tool schema.

browser_navigate.description: Open a URL in the headless browser.

browser_snapshot.description: Accessibility snapshot of the current page — use before click/type to find element refs.

browser_screenshot.description: Visual screenshot of the current page.

browser_click.description: Click an element by CSS selector or accessibility ref.

browser_type.description: Type text into an input field.

browser_press_key.description: Press a keyboard key (Enter, Tab, Escape, etc.).

google_calendar_list.description: List events from Google Calendar for a date range.

google_calendar_create.description: Create a new event on Google Calendar. Ops calendar only unless user explicitly requests primary.

playwright__browser_navigate_back.description: Go back to the previous page.

playwright__browser_snapshot.description: Accessibility snapshot — best for acting on page state.

playwright__browser_file_upload.description: Upload files via a file input.

playwright__browser_handle_dialog.description: Accept or dismiss a browser dialog.

playwright__browser_wait_for.description: Wait for text or a timeout.

playwright__browser_network_requests.description: Return network requests since page load.
